overview: Dredgeless Dredge is a blue-black Pioneer archetype that leverages graveyard recursion and sacrifice synergies to generate overwhelming value. By cycling cheap creatures like Bloodghast and Prized Amalgam through the graveyard, the deck creates a self-sustaining engine that outmaneuvers opponents. Narcomoeba serves as the primary catalyst, enabling repeated returns of threats while drawing cards to maintain board control.
win_conditions: Repeatedly casting Bloodghast from the graveyard to apply relentless pressure
Using Prized Amalgam's sacrifice ability to draw into game-ending threats or answers
Activating Narcomoeba to return large creatures and close out games

keep_criteria: Hands containing Narcomoeba or early graveyard recursion
Two or more cheap creatures (Bloodghast, Prized Amalgam)
Hands with early land drops and interaction
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably one of each color or fetch/shock-compatible), at least one discard spell (Thoughtseize/Inquisition), and a recursion enabler (Narcomoeba/Bloodghast).
Prioritize hands that can establish the graveyard quickly or apply early pressure.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking graveyard interaction or sacrifice outlets
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no discard, or only high-cost spells without graveyard support.

early_game: Prioritize playing Narcomoeba to establish recursion
Cast Bloodghast to populate the graveyard
Use Prized Amalgam to stabilize or draw into answers
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize/Inquisition to strip key threats or discard a cheap spell to build the graveyard.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast a cheap threat or recursion enabler, or continue applying discard.
Land sequencing: Prioritize fetching/finding duals or shock lands early to ensure consistent U/B mana, but don't overextend into removal if the board is vulnerable.
mid_game: Activate Narcomoeba to return threats and apply pressure
Sacrifice creatures to fuel Amalgam's draw ability
Maintain board presence while cycling the graveyard
Balance mana between casting threats, recursion, and interaction.
Don't overcommit to the graveyard if you lack recursion payoffs.
Use Narcomoeba to trade efficiently, Bloodghast to stabilize and generate mana, and Prized Amalgam to snowball when the graveyard is full.
Keep hand size optimal by using discard proactively.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the opponent lacks removal.
Hold back if facing sweepers or targeted hate.
late_game: Execute recursive loops to outvalue opponents
Return larger threats to close the game
Manage card advantage to withstand sweepers
Prized Amalgam with multiple instant/sorcery counters, Bloodghast armies, or Narcomoeba swarms.
Win by overwhelming the board or trading up into unblockable threats.
Use discard to strip win conditions and force unfavorable trades.
Go all-in when the opponent has no removal, no graveyard hate, or when you can safely recurse a Prized Amalgam into a lethal threat.
Commit when you have card advantage and can protect your board.
Push through damage when recursion can outpace their life total or removal cycle.

key_cards.name: Bloodghast
key_cards.role: Primary recursive threat and graveyard filler
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice to return from graveyard; cycle repeatedly to apply pressure and fuel other abilities

key_cards.name: Prized Amalgam
key_cards.role: Card advantage engine and sacrifice outlet
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice to draw cards; use to refill hand after activating Narcomoeba or cycling threats

key_cards.name: Narcomoeba
key_cards.notes: Enters tapped; sacrifice to return target creature from graveyard; central to the deck's value loop

sequencing_tips: Activate Narcomoeba before sacrificing creatures to maximize returns
Hold up black mana to protect key activations from removal
Sacrifice Prized Amalgam after drawing to maintain hand size
Cycle Bloodghast immediately after it resolves to keep the graveyard active
Common mistakes: Overcommitting to the graveyard without recursion payoffs, ignoring discard timing, failing to track opponent's hate cards, and overextending into removal.
Unique interactions: Prized Amalgam scales with every instant/sorcery in the graveyard, so even cheap spells like Thoughtseize or discard make it massive.
Bloodghast's mana ability can be used to cast spells in response to removal.
Narcomoeba can be used as a sacrifice target for other synergies.
Always prioritize graveyard consistency over raw card count.

matchups.advice: Go wide early and pressure life total before they stabilize
Use recursion to outvalue and stabilize with repeated threats
Grind out value and close with Narcomoeba returns

Use discard to strip counterspells or win conditions. Cast threats in response to counterspells if possible. Use graveyard recursion to bypass counters since recursion triggers from the graveyard, not the stack. Force them to use removal on your graveyard threats.

Apply early pressure with discard and cheap threats. Don't let them stabilize. Use graveyard hate if they rely on it, otherwise out-value them with recursion. Disrupt their mana base with discard and pressure their early game.

Win the discard war. Prioritize keeping recursion enablers. Use Prized Amalgam to out-value. Track their graveyard hate and adapt. Trade efficiently and don't overcommit to the graveyard without payoffs. Favor games where you can recur multiple threats before they can answer them.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Tormod's Crypt, Relic), board wipes (Supreme Verdict, Wrath of God), and targeted removal (Path to Exile, Murder).
Note when opponents save removal for your key threats.
Track their discard targets to anticipate what they're trying to stop and adjust your graveyard strategy accordingly.
